docs.title: RapidForge
docs.text: A lightweight Luau utility library for Roblox development.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Require the main module at the top of your script:

local RapidForge = require(game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage"):WaitForChild("library"):WaitForChild("RapidForge"))

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: All functions are documented inside the docs/ folder. See the index below for a full overview.

docs.title: Docs
docs.text: File Description docs/INDEX.md Full function index docs/GUI.md GuiObject manipulation, visibility, cloning, theming docs/ANIMATION.md Fade, slide, and tween helpers docs/PLAYER.md Local player, character, humanoid, inventory docs/WORLD.md Instance traversal, cloning, destruction, model utils docs/MATH.md Lerp, clamp, random, distance, CFrame helpers docs/UTILITY.md Debounce, throttle, table helpers, formatting docs/EVENTS.md Event connection helpers docs/LOGGER.md Structured logging docs/SERVICE.md Service indexing docs/AUDIO.md Background music, playback, volume, and fade.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: File Description examples/healthbar.md Healthbar with RapidForge examples/ChestInteraction.md Chest Interactions with RapidForge examples/EnemySpawner.md Enemy Spawning with RapidForge examples/LeaderboardGui.md Guis with RapidForge

docs.title: Project
docs.text: File Description meta/changelog.md Version history and release notes meta/todo.md Planned functions and improvements meta/contributing.md How to contribute to RapidForge meta/architecture.md RapidForge's architecture

docs.title: Adding Your Own Functions
docs.text:
1. Create a new ModuleScript inside the Library folder with a unique name.
  2. Open RapidForge.lua and register it:
RapidForge.YourFunction = require(Library.YourFunction)

If your module exposes multiple functions:

local yourModule = require(Library.YourModule)
RapidForge.SubFunctionA = yourModule.SubFunctionA
RapidForge.SubFunctionB = yourModule.SubFunctionB

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Have a suggestion or want to share a function? Share it in Discussions

docs.title: License
docs.text: Released into the public domain under the CC0 1.0 Universal.

docs.title: Animation
docs.text: Tween-based animation helpers for GuiObjects.

docs.title: animateFadeIn(instance, duration?)
docs.text:
Fades a GuiObject in by tweening BackgroundTransparency from 1 to 0. Sets Visible to true before playing.

Parameters instance: GuiObject, duration?: number = 0.3 Returns Tween 
RapidForge.animateFadeIn(frame, 0.5)

docs.title: animateFadeOut(instance, duration?)
docs.text:
Fades a GuiObject out by tweening BackgroundTransparency to 1. Sets Visible to false when complete.

Parameters instance: GuiObject, duration?: number = 0.3 Returns Tween 
RapidForge.animateFadeOut(frame, 0.5)

docs.title: animateSlideIn(instance, direction, duration?)
docs.text:
Slides a GuiObject in from a given direction using its current Position as the target. Sets Visible to true before playing.

Parameters instance: GuiObject, direction: string, duration?: number = 0.4 Returns Tween

Valid directions: "left", "right", "top", "bottom"

RapidForge.animateSlideIn(panel, "left", 0.4)

Note: Set the GuiObject's final Position in Studio before calling this. The function reads that position as the slide target and offsets from it.

docs.title: tweenObject(instance, properties, duration?, easingStyle?, easingDirection?, repeats?, reverse?, delay?)
docs.text:
Low-level tween wrapper. Creates and plays a tween on any instance.

Parameters instance: Instance, properties: table, duration?: number = 1, easingStyle?: Enum.EasingStyle = Linear, easingDirection?: Enum.EasingDirection = Out, repeats?: number = 0, reverse?: boolean = false, delay?: number = 0 Returns Tween 
RapidForge.tweenObject(frame, { Size = UDim2.new(1, 0, 1, 0) }, 0.5, Enum.EasingStyle.Quad)

docs.title: Audio
docs.text: Background music management with queue, playback control, volume, and fade support.

docs.title: addToQueue(sound)
docs.text:
Adds a Sound instance to the playback queue.

Parameters sound: Sound 
RapidForge.addToQueue(soundInstance)

docs.title: clearQueue()
docs.text:
Clears all sounds from the queue and resets the index.

RapidForge.clearQueue()

docs.title: play()
docs.text:
Resumes a paused sound or starts playback from the current queue position.

RapidForge.play()

docs.title: stop()
docs.text:
Stops the current sound.

RapidForge.stop()

docs.title: pause()
docs.text:
Pauses the current sound.

RapidForge.pause()

docs.title: skip()
docs.text:
Skips to the next sound in the queue. Respects shuffle mode.

RapidForge.skip()

docs.title: previous()
docs.text:
Goes back to the previous sound in the queue.

RapidForge.previous()

docs.title: setVolume(volume)
docs.text:
Sets the volume of the current sound.

Parameters volume: number (0–1) 
RapidForge.setVolume(0.5)

docs.title: fadeIn(duration?, targetVolume?)
docs.text:
Fades the current sound in from 0 to targetVolume.

Parameters duration?: number = 1, targetVolume?: number = 1 
RapidForge.fadeIn(2, 0.8)

docs.title: fadeOut(duration?, callback?)
docs.text:
Fades the current sound out to 0, then stops it. Calls callback when complete.

Parameters duration?: number = 1, callback?: function 
RapidForge.fadeOut(1, function()
    print("faded out")
end)

docs.title: setLooping(bool)
docs.text:
Enables or disables looping of the current track. When true, the current track repeats instead of advancing the queue.

Parameters bool: boolean 
RapidForge.setLooping(true)

docs.title: setShuffle(bool)
docs.text:
Enables or disables shuffle mode. When true, the next track is picked randomly.

Parameters bool: boolean 
RapidForge.setShuffle(true)

docs.title: isPlaying()
docs.text:
Returns true if a sound is currently playing.

Returns boolean 
if RapidForge.isPlaying() then
    -- music is active
end

docs.title: getCurrent()
docs.text:
Returns the currently active Sound instance.

Returns Sound? 
local sound = RapidForge.getCurrent()

docs.title: Events
docs.text: Helpers for connecting to and managing Roblox events.

docs.title: onEvent(instance, event, callback)
docs.text:
Connects a callback to an event on an instance. A shorthand for instance[event]:Connect(callback).

Parameters instance: Instance, event: string, callback: function Returns RBXScriptConnection 
local conn = RapidForge.onEvent(part, "Touched", function(hit)
    print(hit.Name)
end)

Store the returned connection to disconnect later:

conn:Disconnect()

docs.title: waitFor(parent, name, timeout?)
docs.text:
Waits for a child with the given name to appear under parent. Prints a warning if the timeout is reached without finding it.

Parameters parent: Instance, name: string, timeout?: number = 10 Returns Instance? 
local button = RapidForge.waitFor(screenGui, "PlayButton", 5)

docs.title: GUI
docs.text: Helpers for finding, modifying, and managing GuiObjects.

docs.title: getGui(name)
docs.text:
Returns a ScreenGui by name from the local player's PlayerGui.

Parameters name: string Returns ScreenGui? 
local hud = RapidForge.getGui("HUD")

docs.title: setVisible(instance, bool)
docs.text:
Sets the Visible property on any GuiObject.

Parameters instance: GuiObject, bool: boolean 
RapidForge.setVisible(frame, false)

docs.title: clearChildren(parent)
docs.text:
Destroys all children of a GuiObject.

Parameters parent: GuiObject 
RapidForge.clearChildren(listFrame)

docs.title: cloneInto(template, parent)
docs.text:
Clones a template instance and parents it to the given parent. Returns the clone.

Parameters template: Instance, parent: Instance Returns Instance 
local row = RapidForge.cloneInto(template, scrollFrame)

docs.title: setTextAll(parent, text)
docs.text:
Sets Text on all TextLabel, TextButton, and TextBox descendants inside a parent.

Parameters parent: Instance, text: string 
RapidForge.setTextAll(dialog, "")

docs.title: centerObject(instance)
docs.text:
Sets AnchorPoint to (0.5, 0.5) and Position to UDim2.new(0.5, 0, 0.5, 0).

Parameters instance: GuiObject 
RapidForge.centerObject(popup)

docs.title: setTheme(instance, theme)
docs.text:
Applies a color theme table to a GuiObject and all its descendants. Only sets properties that exist on each descendant's class.

Parameters instance: GuiObject, theme: table

Supported theme keys:

Key Applies to BackgroundColor3 All GuiObject descendants TextColor3 TextLabel, TextButton, TextBox 
RapidForge.setTheme(frame, {
    BackgroundColor3 = Color3.fromRGB(30, 30, 30),
    TextColor3 = Color3.fromRGB(255, 255, 255),
})

docs.title: RapidForge Docs
docs.text:
RapidForge is a lightweight Roblox utility library. Require it once and access all helpers through a single table.

local RapidForge = require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Library.RapidForge)

docs.title: Categories
docs.text: File Description GUI.md GuiObject manipulation, visibility, cloning, theming ANIMATION.md Fade, slide, and tween helpers PLAYER.md Local player, character, humanoid, inventory WORLD.md Instance traversal, cloning, destruction, model utils MATH.md Lerp, clamp, random, distance, CFrame helpers UTILITY.md Debounce, throttle, table helpers, formatting EVENTS.md Event connection helpers

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text:
• All functions are accessed via RapidForge.*
  • All player functions target Players.LocalPlayer unless a player argument is specified
  • Optional parameters are shown with a default value: duration?: number = 0.3
  • Functions that return nothing are omitted from the Returns row

docs.title: Logger
docs.text: Structured logging with levels and per-instance prefixes.

docs.title: Logger.new(prefix?)
docs.text:
Creates a new logger instance. The prefix appears in every message from that logger.

Parameters prefix?: string Returns Logger 
local log = RapidForge.Logger.new("Shop")

docs.title: Logger.setMinLevel(level)
docs.text:
Sets the global minimum log level. Messages below this level are silently ignored.

Parameters level: string — "debug", "info", "warn", "error" 
RapidForge.Logger.setMinLevel("warn") -- hides debug and info

Level order: debug < info < warn < error

Default minimum level is "info".

docs.title: logger:debug(...)
docs.text:
Prints a debug message. Ignored unless min level is "debug".

log:debug("cache hit", itemId)

docs.title: logger:info(...)
docs.text:
Prints an info message.

log:info("player purchased", itemName)

docs.title: logger:warn(...)
docs.text:
Emits a warning via Roblox warn().

log:warn("item not found", itemId)

docs.title: logger:error(...)
docs.text:
Throws an error via Roblox error().

log:error("critical failure in purchase flow")

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
local log = RapidForge.Logger.new("Combat")

log:info("round started")
log:debug("tick", os.clock())
log:warn("player health low", hp)
log:error("humanoid missing")

Output:

[Combat] [INFO] round started
[Combat] [WARN] player health low 12

docs.title: Math / CFrame
docs.text: Numeric helpers, random utilities, and CFrame shortcuts.

docs.title: lerp(a, b, t)
docs.text:
Linearly interpolates between two numbers.

Parameters a: number, b: number, t: number (0–1) Returns number 
local value = RapidForge.lerp(0, 100, 0.5) -- 50

docs.title: clamp(value, min, max)
docs.text:
Clamps a number between min and max.

Parameters value: number, min: number, max: number Returns number 
local safe = RapidForge.clamp(health, 0, 100)

docs.title: roundTo(value, decimals)
docs.text:
Rounds a number to N decimal places.

Parameters value: number, decimals: number Returns number 
local result = RapidForge.roundTo(3.14159, 2) -- 3.14

docs.title: randomFloat(min, max)
docs.text:
Returns a random float between min and max.

Parameters min: number, max: number Returns number 
local speed = RapidForge.randomFloat(10.0, 20.0)

docs.title: randomInt(min, max)
docs.text:
Returns a random integer between min and max inclusive.

Parameters min: number, max: number Returns number 
local roll = RapidForge.randomInt(1, 6)

docs.title: distanceBetween(partA, partB)
docs.text:
Returns the distance between two parts using their Position.

Parameters partA: BasePart, partB: BasePart Returns number 
local dist = RapidForge.distanceBetween(player.Character.HumanoidRootPart, target)

docs.title: lookAtCFrame(origin, target)
docs.text:
Returns a CFrame positioned at origin and facing target.

Parameters origin: Vector3, target: Vector3 Returns CFrame 
local cf = RapidForge.lookAtCFrame(partA.Position, partB.Position)

docs.title: flattenVector(vector)
docs.text:
Returns a copy of vector with Y set to 0. Useful for horizontal distance checks.

Parameters vector: Vector3 Returns Vector3 
local flat = RapidForge.flattenVector(root.Velocity)

docs.title: angleToTarget(origin, target)
docs.text:
Returns the angle in degrees from origin to target on the XZ plane.

Parameters origin: BasePart, target: BasePart Returns number 
local angle = RapidForge.angleToTarget(root, enemy)

docs.title: isInRange(partA, partB, range)
docs.text:
Returns true if the distance between two parts is within range.

Parameters partA: BasePart, partB: BasePart, range: number Returns boolean 
if RapidForge.isInRange(root, chest, 10) then
    -- open chest
end

docs.title: Player
docs.text: Helpers for accessing local player data, character, humanoid, and inventory. All functions target Players.LocalPlayer unless stated otherwise.

docs.title: getPlayer()
docs.text:
Returns the local player. | | | |---|---| | Returns | Player |

local player = RapidForge.getPlayer()

docs.title: getCharacter()
docs.text:
Returns the local player's character. Waits if it hasn't loaded yet. | | | |---|---| | Returns | Model |

local character = RapidForge.getCharacter()

docs.title: getUserId()
docs.text:
Returns the local player's UserId. | | | |---|---| | Returns | number |

local id = RapidForge.getUserId()

docs.title: getDisplayName()
docs.text:
Returns the local player's DisplayName. | | | |---|---| | Returns | string |

local name = RapidForge.getDisplayName()

docs.title: isAlive()
docs.text:
Returns true if the local player's humanoid exists and has health above zero. | | | |---|---| | Returns | boolean |

if RapidForge.isAlive() then
    -- safe to act
end

docs.title: getHumanoid()
docs.text:
Returns the Humanoid from the local character. | | | |---|---| | Returns | Humanoid? |

local humanoid = RapidForge.getHumanoid()

docs.title: getRootPart()
docs.text:
Returns the HumanoidRootPart from the local character. | | | |---|---| | Returns | BasePart? |

local root = RapidForge.getRootPart()

docs.title: getHealth()
docs.text:
Returns the current Health value of the local player's humanoid. | | | |---|---| | Returns | number? |

local hp = RapidForge.getHealth()

docs.title: getTeam()
docs.text:
Returns the local player's current Team. | | | |---|---| | Returns | Team? |

local team = RapidForge.getTeam()

docs.title: getTool()
docs.text:
Returns the Tool currently equipped by the local character, or nil if none. | | | |---|---| | Returns | Tool? |

local tool = RapidForge.getTool()

docs.title: getBackpack()
docs.text:
Returns the local player's Backpack. | | | |---|---| | Returns | Backpack? |

local backpack = RapidForge.getBackpack()

docs.title: getLeaderstats(player?)
docs.text:
Returns the leaderstats folder from a player. Defaults to the local player. | | | |---|---| | Parameters | player?: Player | | Returns | Folder? |

local stats = RapidForge.getLeaderstats()
local coins = stats and stats.Coins.Value

docs.title: getLeaderstat(name, player?)
docs.text:
Returns a single stat by name from the leaderstats folder. Defaults to the local player. | | | |---|---| | Parameters | name: string, player?: Player | | Returns | ValueBase? |

local stat = RapidForge.getLeaderstat("Coins")
if stat then
    print(stat.Value)
end

docs.title: onDied(callback)
docs.text:
Fires callback when the local player's humanoid dies. Reconnects automatically after each respawn. | | | |---|---| | Parameters | callback: function |

RapidForge.onDied(function()
    print("player died")
end)

docs.title: onRespawned(callback)
docs.text:
Fires callback each time the local player respawns. The initial character load on join is ignored. | | | |---|---| | Parameters | callback: function (character: Model) |

RapidForge.onRespawned(function(character)
    print("respawned", character.Name)
end)

docs.title: Services
docs.text: Helper for retrieving Roblox services by name.

docs.title: indexService(name)
docs.text:
Returns a Roblox service by name. On the server, calls game:GetService() directly. On the client, wraps the call in a pcall to safely handle invalid service names.

Parameters name: string Returns Instance? 
local ReplicatedStorage = RapidForge.indexService("ReplicatedStorage")

docs.title: Utility
docs.text: General-purpose helpers for function control, table manipulation, and formatting.

docs.title: debounce(callback, cooldown)
docs.text:
Wraps a function so it can only fire once per cooldown seconds. Subsequent calls within the cooldown window are ignored.

Parameters callback: function, cooldown: number Returns function 
local onTouch = RapidForge.debounce(function(hit)
    -- handle touch
end, 1)

part.Touched:Connect(onTouch)

docs.title: throttle(callback, interval)
docs.text:
Wraps a function so it fires at most once per interval seconds. Unlike debounce, calls between intervals are silently dropped.

Parameters callback: function, interval: number Returns function 
local onRender = RapidForge.throttle(function()
    -- update UI
end, 0.1)

RunService.RenderStepped:Connect(onRender)

debounce vs throttle: Both limit call frequency. debounce resets its window on every call; throttle fires on a fixed interval and drops everything in between.

docs.title: retry(callback, attempts, interval?)
docs.text:
Calls callback up to attempts times using pcall. Stops and returns the result on the first success. Waits interval seconds between attempts.

Parameters callback: function, attempts: number, interval?: number = 1 Returns any? 
local data = RapidForge.retry(function()
    return DataStore:GetAsync(key)
end, 3, 2)

docs.title: formatNumber(number)
docs.text:
Formats a number with comma separators.

Parameters number: number Returns string 
RapidForge.formatNumber(1000000) -- "1,000,000"

docs.title: formatTime(seconds)
docs.text:
Converts a number of seconds into a MM:SS string.

Parameters seconds: number Returns string 
RapidForge.formatTime(90)  -- "01:30"
RapidForge.formatTime(9)   -- "00:09"

docs.title: tableContains(tbl, value)
docs.text:
Returns true if tbl contains value. Uses sequential iteration, so it works on array-style tables.

Parameters tbl: table, value: any Returns boolean 
local allowed = {"admin", "mod", "owner"}
if RapidForge.tableContains(allowed, role) then
    -- grant access
end

docs.title: tableKeys(tbl)
docs.text:
Returns all keys of a dictionary table as an array.

Parameters tbl: table Returns {any} 
local keys = RapidForge.tableKeys({ a = 1, b = 2 }) -- {"a", "b"}

docs.title: tableLength(tbl)
docs.text:
Returns the number of entries in a dictionary table. Use this instead of # for non-sequential tables.

Parameters tbl: table Returns number 
local count = RapidForge.tableLength({ a = 1, b = 2, c = 3 }) -- 3

docs.title: merge(tableA, tableB)
docs.text:
Returns a new table containing all key-value pairs from both tables. Keys in tableB overwrite keys in tableA.

Parameters tableA: table, tableB: table Returns table 
local defaults = { speed = 16, jump = 50 }
local overrides = { speed = 24 }
local config = RapidForge.merge(defaults, overrides)
-- { speed = 24, jump = 50 }

docs.title: deepCopy(tbl)
docs.text:
Returns a full recursive copy of a table. Nested tables are copied by value, not by reference.

Parameters tbl: table Returns table 
local copy = RapidForge.deepCopy(originalConfig)

docs.title: once(callback)
docs.text:
Wraps a function so it can only ever fire once. All subsequent calls are ignored.

Parameters callback: function Returns function

​```lua local initialize = RapidForge.once(function() -- runs exactly one time end)

part.Touched:Connect(initialize) ​```

docs.title: World / Instances
docs.text: Helpers for traversing, modifying, and managing instances in the game world.

docs.title: getDescendantsOfClass(parent, className)
docs.text:
Returns all descendants of parent that match the given class name.

Parameters parent: Instance, className: string Returns {Instance} 
local parts = RapidForge.getDescendantsOfClass(workspace, "Part")

docs.title: findFirstAncestorOfClass(instance, className)
docs.text:
Walks up the instance tree and returns the first ancestor matching the given class.

Parameters instance: Instance, className: string Returns Instance? 
local model = RapidForge.findFirstAncestorOfClass(part, "Model")

docs.title: destroyAfter(instance, seconds)
docs.text:
Destroys an instance after a delay. Does nothing if the instance is already destroyed.

Parameters instance: Instance, seconds: number 
RapidForge.destroyAfter(effect, 3)

docs.title: isDescendantOf(instance, parent)
docs.text:
Returns true if instance is a descendant of parent.

Parameters instance: Instance, parent: Instance Returns boolean 
local inside = RapidForge.isDescendantOf(part, workspace.Map)

docs.title: cloneAt(template, cframe)
docs.text:
Clones a model and places it at the given CFrame using :PivotTo(). Parents the clone to workspace.

Parameters template: Model, cframe: CFrame Returns Model 
local spawned = RapidForge.cloneAt(Templates.Tree, CFrame.new(0, 0, 0))

docs.title: setAllProperties(parent, className, properties)
docs.text:
Bulk-sets properties on all direct children of parent that match className.

Parameters parent: Instance, className: string, properties: table 
RapidForge.setAllProperties(workspace.Platforms, "Part", {
    Anchored = true,
    CanCollide = false,
})

docs.title: countDescendants(parent, className)
docs.text:
Returns the count of all descendants of parent matching the given class.

Parameters parent: Instance, className: string Returns number 
local count = RapidForge.countDescendants(workspace, "SpawnLocation")

docs.title: getRandomChild(parent)
docs.text:
Returns a random direct child of parent. Returns nil if the parent has no children.

Parameters parent: Instance Returns Instance? 
local spot = RapidForge.getRandomChild(workspace.SpawnPoints)

docs.title: moveToSurface(part, targetPart)
docs.text:
Moves part so it sits flush on top of targetPart based on both parts' Y sizes.

Parameters part: BasePart, targetPart: BasePart 
RapidForge.moveToSurface(crate, platform)

docs.title: getModelSize(model)
docs.text:
Returns the bounding box size and CFrame of a model.

Parameters model: Model Returns size: Vector3, cframe: CFrame 
local size, cf = RapidForge.getModelSize(workspace.Castle)
